Football.title: Italy 2 Northern Ireland 0: Sandro Tonali and Prem flop put Azzurri 90 minutes away from World Cup 2026
Football.content:
ITALY are one game away from the World Cup after goals from Sandro Tonali and Moise Keane helped them overcome Northern Ireland 2-0 in the play-off semi-final.

The Azzurri will now play Wales for the chance of reaching their first World Cup since 2014.





Gennaro Gattuso’s side had a nervy time against Michael O’Neill’s men and had to wait until the second to go ahead.

Newcastle star Tonali was the man who broke the deadline with a thunderous drive from just outside the box.

And Italy made sure of their victory after ex-Everton man Kean latched onto a long ball in the area before lashing into the corner with just minutes to go.

More to follow.

Football.title: ‘Mikel was our boss’ – Ex-Arsenal star reveals how Arteta took over from Arsene Wenger when he was still a player
Football.content:
MIKEL ARTETA was already taking over Arsenal’s pre-match tactics as a player, years before he became manager at the club.

That’s according to Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain, who has revealed that the Arsenal players saw Arteta as “the boss” ahead of big games despite Arsene Wenger still being in charge.





Oxlade-Chamberlain and Arteta spent five years together on the books at Arsenal, helping the Gunners to two FA Cups as teammates.

That was over 10 years ago now, but even back then, Oxlade-Chamberlain could see that Arteta had the credentials to be a manager.

Despite Wenger being in charge at the time, Oxlade-Chamberlain revealed that Arteta would lead tactical meetings about the opposition ahead of big games.

Speaking on In The Mixer, brought to you by Sky Bet, Oxlade-Chamberlain said: “He [Arteta] started analysis meetings for us because Arsene comes from a different generation.



“It was all about us, and if we take care of what we’re meant to take care of, we should win.

“But when it got to the point where you’re playing you boys [Man City] or we have a big game, Mikel was our boss.

“We want to know what they’re doing from a corner. We didn’t really do that.

“So Mikel sort of forced that in as a player that we started watching the opposition more, whoever it was we wanted to know.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“Because before we just see it on the paper, on the board and the boss [Wenger] would just start drawing lines.”

Arteta has now gone on to be one of the Premier League’s best managers, and is attempting to lead his Arsenal side to a historic treble this season.

The Gunners are nine points clear at the top of the table and have secured their spot in the quarter-finals of both the Champions League and the FA Cup too.

Oxlade-Chamberlain recently returned to Arsenal, training with Arteta’s side to keep fit after leaving Besiktas in the summer.



The Full Arsenal on YouTube SunSport have launched a dedicated Arsenal channel on YouTube! The Full Arsenal Podcast is your home for everything about the Gunners. From match reactions and tactical breakdowns to transfer talk, match previews, tactical analysis and honest fan debate… The Full Arsenal covers it all. Subscribe to The Full Arsenal here

The 32-year-old managed to secure a move to Celtic off the back of his sessions at Arsenal, but was left impressed by the level of detail he saw from Arteta’s tactics at the training ground.

He added: “I think now you can see, and obviously I’ve just trained with them boys before I went to Celtic for three months and some of the stuff I was seeing and learning, it was an eye opener.

“For me, it’s what I always imagined that Pep [Guardiola]’s team was operating on.

“Because even at Liverpool, we were maybe a bit more simple and our philosophies were different, but I can kind of see that stuff that Mikel’s doing.



“Every detail really is crazy. And as a player, he probably took more pride in that than the average player while still playing.”

Football.title: Luke Littler urges Germany star ‘please come to Man Utd’ and admits ‘we need help’ with Casemiro leaving
Football.content:
DARTS star Luke Littler has urged Stuttgart midfielder Angelo Stiller to join his beloved Manchester United.

The world champion was competing against Stephen Bunting in Night 8 of the Premier League Darts in Berlin.





He managed a 6-3 win over his British rival to secure a semi-final tie against Gerwyn Price.

But before his victory, he was quizzed by German reporter Leon Karasch, who turned his attentions to football.

The reporter decided to ask Littler who his favourite German footballer was after flying in to the capital.

Littler picked out Stiller, who has been linked with Old Trafford in the past after his impressive displays in the Bundesliga.

He also claimed that United “need help” because Brazilian veteran Casemiro was leaving on a free transfer in the summer.

The world no 1 replied: “I’ll say Angelo Stiller. Come to United! Please come. We need help. Casemiro is leaving us.”

Karasch was impressed by Littler’s response and praised his “football knowledge”.

Littler is a huge Red Devils fan and paraded his World Darts Championship trophy around Old Trafford last month.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

And in December he was seen in the away end as United secured a 4-1 win at Wolves.

He is a big football fan in general and paid a visit to England’s training ground this week.

Littler put on a darts tournament and sent a good luck message to the squad ahead of their World Cup this summer.



Stiller, the player he picked out, has one goal and seven assists for Stuttgart this season.

He has played 124 times for Stuttgart since joining in 2023 and has scored a total of six goals.

Stiller started his career with Bayern Munich before having a three-year stint with Hoffenheim.

The midfielder was linked with United as recently as October, when United were said to be scouting the highly-rated star.

At the time, it was reported that Stiller has a release clause in his contract that Stuttgart can buy him out of for £2million.



Sky Sport Germany reported that Stuttgart intend to ask for more than £43m for Stiller, who was said to be keen to move.

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel reveals ‘tough’ conversation with NINE England stars set to be sent home after Uruguay clash
Football.content:
THOMAS TUCHEL has revealed that he has had “tough” conversations with NINE stars who are set to be sent home.

The England manager has confirmed that there are nine players who are likely to leave the Three Lions camp after the friendly against Uruguay.



England host the South Americans at Wembley on Friday evening in their penultimate match before the World Cup.



Tuchel, 52, called up 35 players for the clash as well as the following friendly versus Japan on March 31.

The bloated squad will be chopped down for the final 26 to travel for the tournament across the US, Canada and Mexico.

The former Chelsea and Bayern Munich boss has already warned the nine players who are likely to go home.

Tuchel said: “I think the tough part is already done. I told them already, the candidates.

“They know that it is possible. And I think everyone — including myself — had a chance to digest it.

“I’ve told 15-year-olds they cannot stay at the academy level when I was U15, U14 coach.

“That was really hard because they start crying after five seconds and they feel that their life dream is shattered by their coach.”

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

Tuchel has also pleaded with England fans to avoid jeering Benjamin White as he insisted the defender deserves a second chance for the Three Lions.



The Arsenal man is in the squad for the first time since he left the 2022 World Cup in Qatar due to personal reasons.

Tuchel said: “I can imagine there are some strong opinions out there, but nobody in camp has had the strongest of opinions, so yes, he deserves a second chance.

“People have to trust me that he behaves very, very well. He was positive about coming back and was desperate for many months to come back.

“Now he has the chance because Jarell Quansah pulled out through injury and he is eager to prove a point. Hopefully people give him a fair chance.”



White, 28, who has four senior caps, left Qatar due to a number of reasons which included a bust-up with England assistant Steve Holland.

Asked if he had spoken to White about his reasons for quitting the World Cup, Tuchel said: “I asked him and he didn’t want to speak about it. I didn’t want to know about it because I think he deserves a second chance.

“He has not put a foot wrong with me. Something must have happened, because if a player leaves from a tournament, it’s never good and it never can be nothing.

“I’m not sure if ‘apologise’ is the right word, but maybe just to clear the air with the players who were there, and I think he has done that with the majority of the players.”

Tuchel was also asked why he picked White and not Trent Alexander-Arnold.



He said: “It’s just a choice. A sporting choice, a difficult choice, and maybe a hard choice, and maybe to a certain degree unfair. But these choices have to be made.

“The choice was for Ben White because I saw him play here in the Carabao Cup final against Manchester City.

“He played the Champions League match I think before and was straightaway back to his old self.”



Even though John Stones is struggling with a calf injury, White is set to start on the substitute bench.

After Friday’s game, Tuchel will send home nine of the squad with another 11 stars linking up for Tuesday’s home game with Japan.

One player with much to prove is Cole Palmer who last played for England against Andorra last June, his only appearance for Tuchel.

Palmer has struggled with form and fitness for Chelsea.

Tuchel said: “He had a difficult season but he has also had a difficult spell with the national team.



“He was only once available for us and when he was available, we decided to stick with the same squad so there is big competition for his best position, number 10.”

Jude Bellingham, who has been sidelined for Real Madrid with a hamstring injury, also trained with the squad at Wembley last night but he will not feature but should play against Japan.

Tuchel added: “He will get some minutes in the second game. We don’t take any risks.

I was surprised that Madrid took the risk in the last game but we won’t take the risk.”

Football.title: England’s biggest fan reveals he’s selling his house to fund seven-week World Cup bonanza
Football.content:
ENGLAND superfan Andy Milne has revealed he will sell his house to fund his trip to the World Cup this summer.

The ex-teacher, 62, has become one of the most famous Three Lions supporters after gaining publicity at Qatar 2022.





Milne has already booked his flights to the United States for this year’s tournament and will be going to every England game.

But his plan to be out in the States for seven weeks will come at a huge cost after eye-watering prices for the World Cup were revealed.

This year’s World Cup is the most expensive in history after fans complained about sky-high prices for flights, tickets and hotels.

England fans will have to fork out more than £5,000 to watch every game should the team reach the final.

They are faced with paying a minimum of £526 just to watch all three of their group games against Croatia, Ghana and Panama.

And Milne is taking drastic measures to ensure he can follow England throughout the tournament.

He has decided to put his second home in Northwich, Cheshire, on the market for £350,000 to help pay for his trip.

Milne usually rents out the property but he said it “felt like the right time to cash in” after owning it for 27 years.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

He told the Mirror: “It is going on the market because I’m selling it to go to the World Cup.

“The last tournament in Qatar was a present to myself, I had been saving for years. We have had a second home for 27 years so it felt like the right time to cash in.

“I definitely want to see the whole tournament. I am going to the US on June 3 and will be there for seven weeks. So it will cost quite a lot of money.



“I always try to do things cheaply if I can. I will couch surf where possible; I am lucky to have friends in Mexico, Dallas, and Vancouver.

“Travel costs are expensive; I bought international flights early because you can change them even if departure times change by 10 minutes.”

Milne went viral after a picture circulated showing him holding a replica of the World Cup trophy.

Some fans claimed he looked like former Three Lions boss Steve McClaren.

Milne admitted he takes the World Cup replica with him during his trips after fellow fans began to ask him for selfies with it.



His first game will be in Dallas for England’s tournament opener against Croatia on June 17.

Football.title: Paul Scholes names Premier League Mount Rushmore as Paddy McGuinness intervenes – ‘quite a f***ing red mountain’
Football.content:
THE age old debate has reared it’s ugly head again: which four players make the Mt. Rushmore of the Premier League?

Two players certainly qualified to give their opinion are Paul Scholes and Nicky Butt and the ex-Manchester United stars gave their opinion sitting opposite Paddy McGuiness on their podcast The Good, The Bad and The Football.





Scholes started: “Roy Keane. Ryan Giggs. Wayne Rooney.

McGuiness interrupted: “Can I just stop you there…”

Butt asked: “Is this a Man United one?”

McGuiness: “I’ve got to say, it’s quite a f***ing red mountain this.”



Scholes: “Well, the red mountain won a lot of Premier League trophies. [Last pick] Thierry Henry.”

McGuiness: “F*** me.

Butt: “All joking aide, as you’ve said before, it’s impossible these. F***ing stupid these quizzes, stop doing them.”

Butt went on to say it’s all about personal choice before stating Steven Gerrard not being named would be an omission to some and Scholes admitted the same goes for Alan Shearer.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Butt: “It’s only because I’ve played with Roy [Keane] so many times. I could have easily picked Scholesy, I could have easily picked Gerrard.

“I can’t say Gerrard was better than Scholesly or that Scholesy was better than… it’s the three of them.

McGuiness: “If you look at someone like Lampard as well, with his goals…”

Butt: “Lampard was unbelieveable.”

Scholes: “But when you’re talking about Mt Rushmore, you’ve got to talk about people who have won Premier Leagues. You’ve gotta do.”



As someone who won the Premier League eleven times, Scholes is well within his right to think that matters when deciding the four GOATS of the Premier League.

Gerrard, though a wonderful player who led Liverpool to win just about everything else including a Champions League, never did win the title despite being a league-leading player for over a decade.

Butt was clearly talking in terms of who the better players were and how inseperable the likes of Keane, Scholes and Gerrard were, but as Scholes suggests, you have to find ways to divide them when you can only have four.

Football.title: How Everton could be RELEGATED from Champions League if David Moyes’ side finish season in style and qualify
Football.content:
EVERTON’S Champions League dream could be turned into a nightmare even if they finish the season in style.

David Moyes‘ side are outsiders to gatecrash Europe’s elite competition but still have a fighting chance of beating bitter rivals Liverpool to a top five spot.





Everton have won three of their last five Premier League games to close the gap on the top five to just three points.

The Toffees are also competing with Brentford and Chelsea for the final UCL spot.

And their next two games in the league could be crucial as they face the Bees before hosting Liverpool.

If the Merseyside outfit managed to qualify, then they would have a first European tour to look forward to since 2017/18.

That season saw the club compete in the Europa League, while the last time they were in the Champions League was in the 2005/06 season, when they lost in the qualifying round.

But there is a chance Everton could be booted out of the competition depending on what happens in Serie A.

The Goodison Park side are owned by Dan Friedkin — who also holds the keys at Italian giants Roma.

Like Everton, Roma are just three points away from the Champions League places as they trail fourth-placed Como 1907.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET UP TO £100 BONUS

Uefa does not want clubs with the same owner participating in the same competition in the same season.

Therefore, if both teams were to climb into the UCL places in their respective divisions, then one would be dropped into the Europa League.

The same would happen if they both finish in spots to qualify for the Europa League – as one would be lowered into the Conference League.

If both of them finished in the Conference League spots, then one would be out of Europe altogether.

Whichever team finished in a better position in their domestic league would get to keep their spot in the higher competition.



That’s why, last summer, Crystal Palace were dropped into the Conference League as they could not compete in the Europa League with Lyon.

This decision saw Nottingham Forest promoted from the Europe’s third tier competition to the Europa League.

Therefore, if Everton finished in 5th and Roma in 4th, then the spot in the Champions League would go to Gian Piero Gasperini’s side due to the higher spot.



But, critically, if the two teams finish in the SAME league position domestically, then the side from the highest-ranked nation would get preference — in this case Everton.

There is a precedent of clubs getting around the rules as Girona’s ownership was given over to independent trustees so they could compete in the Champions League.

However, there is no sign that the option has been considered by the Friedkins as of yet.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Roma competed in the Europa League this season but were dumped out in the round of 16.

They came off second best in an all Italian affiar against Bologna, with the aggregate score ending 5-4 in favour of the Emilia-Romagna-based side.

Football.title: Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain reveals moment he knew he had to leave Arsenal when career started ‘going backwards’
Football.content:
ALEX OXLADE-CHAMBERLAIN has admitted that “no part” of him wanted to leave Arsenal, but felt he had to after being pushed to wing-back.

Oxlade-Chamberlain, 32, left Arsenal in 2017, joining Liverpool in a deal worth £35million.





The 35-time England international is now playing at Celtic, following a two-year stint in Turkey with Besiktas.

Oxlade-Chamberlain was tipped for big things when he first arrived in North London in 2011, aged 17, swapping Southampton for the capital in a £12m transfer.

While on the books with the Gunners, Oxlade-Chamberlain made 198 appearances and won three FA Cups, but he still felt like he had to move on in order to keep his career moving in the right direction.

Speaking on In The Mixer, brought to you by Sky Bet, he said: “It was a really tough decision for me at the time because there was no part of me that wanted to leave Arsenal.



“I loved it there and I was really comfortable there. I was seven years in. But the overriding thing was I was always told I’m going into midfield and it was always next season, ‘we’re going to do it next season’.”

Oxlade-Chamberlain felt the game was moving in a direction that put more impetus on goalscoring stats, something that he thought wasn’t a strong part of his game when he played on the wing.

And the former Arsenal man had been told by Arsene Wenger, when he initially signed for Arsenal, that he would be moved into midfield as part of his development – a promise that didn’t come to fruition.

He explained: “I knew the game was going [in the direction of] more stats, like goals and assist numbers.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“I’m thinking I’m not going to be getting the right numbers here to be talked about at the right level and I know I want to be a midfielder. The boss has said I am a midfielder.

“When he [Wenger] signed me, that was another reason why I went there. He said. ‘I can tell you’re a central-midfielder and I’m going to bring that in but you’re going to start as a winger.’

“So that’s one of the reasons why I went to Arsenal because he saw that for me as well. But then every year we had so many good midfielders.

“I would play one game in midfield, he couldn’t drop me but he also needs to get Mikel Arteta, Aaron Ramsey, Jack Wilshere or Santi Cazorla into midfield. So I’d go back to the wing again.



“I never got a consistent run in that position. And then we went to five at the back and I ended up at wing back and I thought, ‘oh no, this is going the wrong direction, going backwards’.”

Liverpool came knocking and Oxlade-Chamberlain was offered a role as a No8 under Jurgen Klopp, an opportunity he couldn’t turn down given the circumstances.

From match reactions and tactical breakdowns to transfer talk, match previews, tactical analysis and honest fan debate… The Full Arsenal covers it all. Subscribe to The Full Arsenal here

He admitted that the choice to join Liverpool was one of “head over heart”, but things didn’t work out for him in terms of game time when he swapped the Emirates for Anfield.

Despite spending the same amount of time on Merseyside as he had in N5, Oxlade-Chamberlain made far less appearances after battling with injuries.



A torn ACL led to more issues with his knees, ankles and hamstrings and the midfielder missed nearly 600 DAYS of action.

But he was still able to play his part in winning the Premier League and Champions League under Klopp.

Now the experienced pro is playing in the Scottish Premiership for Celtic, who he joined on a short-term deal last month.

Since his arrival at Celtic Park, Oxlade-Chamberlain has made seven appearances and scored one goal.

Football.title: David Beckham showed true colours with gesture to ex-Arsenal star after horror injury
Football.content:
David Beckham showed true colours with gesture to ex-Arsenal star after horror injury

Aaron Ramsey remains without a club following his exit from Mexican side Pumas UNAM earlier this season but has opened up on a heartwarming gift from England icon David Beckham

View 2 Images David Beckham sent Ramsey a shirt after his leg break (Image: Getty Images)

Aaron Ramsey has revealed that David Beckham sent him a signed shirt after his shocking leg break. The Welsh midfielder sustained a double fracture in his right leg back in 2010 against Stoke City.

‌



In all, he was out of action for nine months, with Ramsey undergoing an intensive period of rehabilitation. Over 15 years since his injury, the former Arsenal man has revealed that he was sent a touching present by England and Manchester United icon Beckham.

‌



Asked if he collects shirts, Ramsey said: “I’ve got a couple of shirts that really mean a lot to me. The one that stands out for me though, is I’ve got a David Beckham shirt personalised when I broke my leg.

‌



“He wrote a lovely message on the back of his shirt, it was a Milan shirt. That’s definitely one that meant a lot and especially going through what I was going through at the time.”

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Julian Alvarez plan becomes clear amid Arsenal transfer interest as Atletico Madrid set for talks READ MORE :

Noni Madueke in no mood to 'sulk' as he bids to nail down World Cup place READ MORE :

Ramsey went on to make a full recovery and eventually made 369 appearances for Arsenal, scoring 64 goals. He helped the North London side to three FA Cups and two Community Shields during his time at the Emirates Stadium before a free transfer to Italian giants Juventus.

‌



Ramsey spent three seasons contracted to Juventus ahead of moves to Rangers, Nice, Cardiff City and Pumas UNAM. He saw his deal with the latter ripped up earlier this season.

Speaking at the time, Ramsey said: “I would like to thank the Mexican people for their kindness and generosity and making me and my family feel so welcome in Mexico City. I also want to give a big thanks to the manager, the coaching staff and particularly the medical staff for helping me through what was a difficult time with injuries.

“However with their help and a lot of hard work I was ready and match fit for the remaining games and I was looking forward to giving my best to the Pumas. Unfortunately my contract was terminated which was a surprise and obviously disappointing.

‌



View 2 Images Aaron Ramsey is currently without a club (Image: Getty Images)

“Therefore I had no choice but to come home to Wales. Thank you again for all the support through the time we spent in Mexico. It’s a beautiful place and we will definitely come back to visit soon.”

As he continues his search for a new club, Ramsey has admitted that he is open to moving into coaching. Speaking to talkSPORT, he said: “Yes, absolutely. That's a route that I never thought I'd take, but definitely in the last few years I've done my badges, I'm on the Pro (License Course) at the moment.

‌



“It's something that I really enjoy doing with Cardiff Under-18s at the beginning of that and then obviously I took charge of Cardiff for the last three games which was really good, a great experience.

“I went with Wales last week and that’s a real good insight into everything that goes on behind the scenes. The hours and the effort and detail that goes into those things and preparing the boys to go into those things to execute a gameplan. But it’s addictive, so I definitely see myself going into that space.”

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel makes Ben White plea to England fans as Arsenal star makes Three Lions return
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel makes Ben White plea to England fans as Arsenal star makes Three Lions return

Thomas Tuchel has pleaded with England fans not to boo Ben White on his return to the international fold after he walked out of the World Cup in 2022

View Image White is in line to make his England return against Uruguay (Image: PA)

Thomas Tuchel has urged fans not to boo Ben White on his England return. But Tuchel also admitted the Arsenal defender owes his teammates an explanation ahead of his first cap since he walked out on England at the 2022 World Cup.

‌



White was called up to the England squad for the friendlies with Uruguay and Japan to end his three-and-a-half-year exile.

‌



England boss Tuchel insisted White was desperate to join up despite the controversy which saw the 27-year-old leave the camp and he has never publicly explained his version of events.

‌



When asked what he hopes from the England fans, Tuchel said: “Support, because he plays for England and we play in a positive moment. We will have some new players on the pitch so I hope everyone can support.

“I can imagine there are some strong opinions out there, but nobody is in camp who has made the strongest opinions, so yeah, he deserves a second chance.

Thomas Tuchel warns England hopefuls they are in last-chance saloon for World Cup spot READ MORE :

Noni Madueke in no mood to 'sulk' as he bids to nail down World Cup place READ MORE :

“People have to trust me that he behaves very, very well. He was positive about coming back and was desperate for many months to come back. Now he has the chance

‌



“If you believe it or not, I didn’t hear a story about it. I asked him and he didn’t want to speak about it. I didn’t want to know about it because I think he deserves a second chance. I want to give him a second chance.

“He has not put a foot wrong with me. Something must have happened, because if a player leaves from a tournament it’s never good and it never can be nothing.

‌



“I’m not sure if ‘apologise’ is the right word, but maybe just to clear the air with the players who were there, I think he has done and will do with the majority of the players who come now. He trains on a very high level, exceptionally good in training.

“The reaction to his call-up was very positive. Within seconds, it was a very positive and emotional reaction from him. He made it very clear how delighted he is to be back. This is what he has shown in the last days.

“Everyone deserves a second chance, but I don’t even know if he did something wrong or what exactly happened. But an explanation among the players who were there, to clear the air, is sure in progress.”

Article continues below

Meanwhile, England defender John Stones is a fitness doubt for the Uruguay game with a calf problem.

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel warns England hopefuls they are in last-chance saloon for World Cup spot
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel warns England hopefuls they are in last-chance saloon for World Cup spot

Some of England's fringe stars will get the opportunity to impress Thomas Tuchel for the final time on Friday night as they bid to secure their spots on the plane to the World Cup

View Image Tuchel will run the rule over his fringe stars in the Uruguay friendly (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Thomas Tuchel has warned England’s World Cup hopefuls are facing a last chance saloon at Wembley. Tuchel will field an experimental line-up against Uruguay and for some of the starters it will be their final audition to force their way into the squad for this summer.

‌



England boss Tuchel picked a 35-man squad with several players going home after this weekend to make way for the more established stars to play against Japan next Tuesday.

‌



Tuchel said: “We address the reality and encourage everyone to be the best version of themselves and to enjoy it. It is the last camp before the World Cup. That’s just how it is, and everyone tries to impress.

‌



“We encourage them to be good team-mates and be positive, train with a positive attitude, and connect as fast as possible, because some of them are new, some of them haven’t been in camp for quite a long time. And everyone did this. Very positive about these days together.

BBC and ITV given green light to show World Cup games on YouTube in landmark deal READ MORE :

Noni Madueke in no mood to 'sulk' as he bids to nail down World Cup place READ MORE :

“And hopefully we can show it tomorrow. It will be a new lineup, a new mix of teams. It is not always easy. And then suddenly you get the chance.

“Everything is new, not only for yourself but for your team-mates and you. And this is not always easy but we take it into consideration and go from there, and at the moment we are very positive. Hopefully they can show it tomorrow.”

‌



Tuchel delivered clear messages to Chelsea’s Cole Palmer who has not played for England since last June and also Arsenal defender Ben White.

White is back in the squad after quitting on England during the 2022 World Cup and Tuchel urged the fans not to boo him while he admitted there is “pressure on” Palmer to force his way into the squad.

Tuchel added: “I think he (White) deserves a second chance. I want to give him a second chance. He has not put a foot wrong with me. Something must have happened, because if a player leaves from a tournament it’s never good and it never can be nothing.

‌



“I’m not sure if ‘apologise’ is the right word, but maybe just to clear the air with the players who were there, I think he has done and will do with the majority of the players who come now.

“He plays for England and we play in a positive moment. He deserves a second chance. We will have some new players on the pitch so I hope everyone can support him.

Article continues below

“Honestly, Cole has to show because we have more evidence without him than with him so the pressure is on him. He had a difficult season but he has also had a difficult spell with the national team.”

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel gives response to Trent Alexander-Arnold social media post after England snub
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel gives response to Trent Alexander-Arnold social media post after England snub

Trent Alexander-Arnold took to social media to respond to Thomas Tuchel's omission from the England squad this month

View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold has not been called up to represent England since last summer

Thomas Tuchel has explained his decision to leave Trent Alexander-Arnold out of his England squad for this month's friendlies with Uruguay and Japan. After missing out on a call-up, the Real Madrid star Alexander-Arnold posted the message 'Real Madrid and nothing else.'

‌



The England head coach responded: "As a response to not being nominated? Well fair enough, fair enough. I think it's a very hard decision that we took. No doubt about his talents, no doubt about his career and what he can give to teams.

‌



"We created a slightly different game model when he was not in camp in September, October, November. It was a game model built on intensity, built on positivity, built even like in the profile of the full-back, on overlaps, on underlaps, very intense so it was a slightly different profile of players.

‌



BBC and ITV given green light to show World Cup games on YouTube in landmark deal READ MORE :

Noni Madueke in no mood to 'sulk' as he bids to nail down World Cup place READ MORE :

"We just stick to these guys and I know it's on his back and he was with us in June. Maybe he deserved a second chance. It is a very hard difficult decision, I'm aware of it but he took it on his chin.

"We will keep on going and I will make sure I will see some matches from Real Madrid and maybe a Champions League match live to get my last impressions.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



"He's on the long list and everyone is still in the mix, but at the moment, some other guys are just ahead."

The 27-year-old made headlines after being left out of Tuchel's 35-man Three Lions squad with Tino Livramento and Djed Spence selected ahead of him. The former Liverpool full-back hasn't featured for England since last June during the World Cup qualifiers.

After returning from an ankle injury, Alexander-Arnold played 26 minutes in the Three Lions' narrow 1-0 win over Andorra, before being named on the bench as an unused substitute for the following match - a 3-1 defeat to Senegal in a friendly.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

After missing out on a place at international level, he was dropped to the bench by Madrid boss Alvaro Arbeloa after arriving late to a team meeting ahead of the derby against city rivals Atletico Madrid.

View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold and Thomas Tuchel

‌



Alexander-Arnold's season has been disrupted by a series of injuries, with his settling-in period in the Spanish capital repeatedly interrupted by recurring fitness setbacks.

England will play two matches this month before a final friendly against Costa Rica just days before the World Cup begins. Tuchel has not completely ruled out recalling Alexander-Arnold for the tournament.

Football.title: Noni Madueke in no mood to 'sulk' as he bids to nail down World Cup place
Football.content:
Noni Madueke in no mood to 'sulk' as he bids to nail down World Cup place

Arsenal's Noni Madueke is ready to be the 'difference maker' as he faces three-way battle for his place on the plane to the World Cup

View 3 Images Noni Madueke aiming for England place (Image: Michael Regan/Getty Images)

Noni Madueke insists he is in no mood to “sulk” and is ready for the biggest fight of his career. Madueke has been an important part of Arsenal ’s squad this season but suffered the heartbreak of losing the Carabao Cup final at Wembley.

‌



But Madueke has got used to proving people wrong and battling for his place which will be the case again for a place in England’s World Cup squad.

‌



Gunners winger Madueke is probably up against club teammate Bukayo Saka and Jarrod Bowen with two from three making the plane to North America.

‌



Madueke is set to get his audition against Uruguay on Friday night with Saka one of the eleven England regulars only due to report up for next Tuesday’s friendly with Japan.

BBC and ITV given green light to show World Cup games on YouTube in landmark deal READ MORE :

Wayne Rooney explains the key difference between Thomas Tuchel and Gareth Southgate READ MORE :

It is a challenge that Madueke is ready to embrace as he targets a dream end to the season with a place in the squad and trophies with title-chasing Arsenal as they are still in the hunt for three even after losing to Manchester City on Sunday.

Madueke said: “Listen, my focus now is on England for sure. Of course it's difficult, those type of games but you take it in your stride, you can't sulk, you have to carry on.

‌



“We've got important games for England coming up, which I'm fully focused on to try and do my best and when we get back to club it'll be the final push to try and finish where we want to.

"In terms of my mindset, when you play at the highest level for England and for club, you need to have that mindset of trying to just do your best and compete and help your team win.

“In terms of World Cup places, I don’t really think too much about that. I just try to do my best whenever I’m on the pitch and everything will play out how it’s meant to.

‌



“Of course as a player these things cross your mind. It would be naïve to think it wouldn’t. It’s an amazing stage and an amazing honour to play for your country at a World Cup so, yeah, it definitely has.”

Madueke, 24, has had highs and lows at Arsenal this season. He was brilliant in the autumn when Saka was out injured and that is when he really pushed himself to the fore with England. Saka (who everyone at Arsenal calls B) and Madueke also happen to be great pals.

‌



Madueke’s game time has been much less in recent weeks but he is still a big part of the squad in terms of starts and also making an impact from the bench.

But he definitely knows what he has to do to get a place on the plane because Tuchel has been very clear that he effectively wants two players for each slot.

View 3 Images Noni Madueke ready for England chance (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

‌



Madueke said: “I think for any player it's great to know what you need to do, to know who you're competing against. The manager's made it very clear to all of us. It's fantastic.

“There's great competition for places and that's only going to make the overall level of the group higher. I feel like in that sense he's been really, really, really good with us.

“There’s always an edge in training. It’s one of the most competitive squads in the world with some of the best players in the world so regardless of who is here training is of a very high level.

‌



“We have very high standards for ourselves and each other and that doesn’t change, regardless of the set up and who is here.

“First and foremost with me, Jarrod and Bukayo for England, or me and B for Arsenal, at the end of the day we want the team success first before ourselves. I feel like if you don't want to have that it's hard to really achieve something great.

“That's at the forefront of your minds. And then of course it's normal, you're a competitor, you're trying to play as much as possible. That's just the competition, that's just the nature of the game.”

‌



View 3 Images Madueke is likely to battle Bowen for a place on the plane (Image: Kevin Hodgson/MI News/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Madueke was signed by Arsenal to be a “difference maker” and specifically be the player to unlock a “low block” when facing opponents.

He also feels he has matured as a player and a person since his £50m move to Arsenal this season.

‌



Madueke added: “I think it's clear what my strengths are. Anytime I go onto the pitch, I try and use my strengths to the best of my ability to help the team.

“I think I'm maturing as a player, I'm a little bit older now than when I first played for England and in terms of having an England shirt on, I've always really proud to play for my country and I've always felt really, really good to be in this environment as well.

“I think my performances for England have shown that so I hope I just kick on and continue to do my thing for the country.”

Football.title: Julian Alvarez plan becomes clear amid Arsenal transfer interest as Atletico Madrid set for talks
Football.content:
Julian Alvarez plan becomes clear amid Arsenal transfer interest as Atletico Madrid set for talks

Julian Alvarez has been linked with a move away from Atletico Madrid in recent weeks, with Barcelona and Arsenal both reportedly interested in the Argentine forward

View 2 Images Arsenal transfer target Julian Alvarez has been linked with a return to the Premier League (Image: Europa Press via Getty Images)

Atletico Madrid are reportedly ready to pull out all the stops to persuade Julian Alvarez to remain at the club amid interest from Barcelona and Arsenal. The 26-year-old's future has become a hot topic of late as the summer transfer window looms.

‌



Barcelona have been touted as a potential new home for Alvarez should he leave Atletico, but Arsenal and Chelsea are also said to be long-standing admirers of Alvarez and could be enticed to make a bid. The Gunners were previously interested in signing Alvarez before his move to Atletico in 2024.

‌



However, Atletico, who hold the Argentine in high regard, are willing to make him one of the top earners at the club as an incentive for him to extend his contract, reports Mundo Deportivo. Atletico are now believed to be prepared to meet Alvarez's financial demands after initial hesitation, with a proposed wage of around €10 million (£8m) per year - a rise on the €7m (£6m) he currently pockets.

‌



Man Utd add Arsenal's Myles Lewis Skelly to growing summer transfer shortlist READ MORE :

The Colchoneros are reportedly confident he will pledge his long-term future to the club, a move that would make it significantly more difficult for Barcelona or Arsenal to lure him away.

Alvarez is contracted until 2030 and has a €500m (£432m) release clause, considerably less than the €100m (£87m) Barca are thought to be willing to offer.

The report also highlights that official discussions over a fresh contract have yet to commence, though Atletico remain hopeful that Alvarez and his family's successful settling in Madrid will prove a determining factor.

‌



As for Arsenal, the Mirror's sister title in the capital, football.london, reports that interest is authentic and they are well placed should a chance emerge but any transfer would demand a significant fee, meaning player departures will be essential this summer if they plan to make a serious move.

Atletico manager Diego Simeone, a longstanding admirer of the Argentine, has previously emphasised just how vital the player is to his squad.

"A goal is always an incentive for strikers," he said.

‌



"He's a very important player, recognised worldwide, wanted by lots of teams, but we have him and hopefully he can give us everything he's got, because he's a fantastic footballer."

View 2 Images Diego Simeone has spoken highly of Arsenal transfer target Julian Alvarez

Arsenal invested approximately £250m on seven new arrivals last summer and this time they're anticipated to reduce spending to guarantee they remain compliant with the Premier League's Profitability and Sustainability Rules (PSR).

Football.title: Names and address of 300 'dodgy stick' users to be given to Sky in latest crackdown
Football.content:
Names and address of 300 'dodgy stick' users to be given to Sky in latest crackdown

Premier League fans have been accessing streams of matches not broadcast on UK TV channels via other means but there is now set to be crackdown on the technology

View 2 Images Police are attempting to crack down on the use of illegal Fire sticks and boxes (Image: Getty Images)

Details of over 300 ‘dodgy box’ users will be handed over to Sky after a ruling by the Irish High Court.

‌



Justice Brian Cregan has granted an order that Revolut Bank UAB must provide the names and addresses of 304 subscribers as well as 10 resellers. The order comes after Sky launched a civil action against Revolut, with subscribers using the banking app to pay resellers of pirated content.

‌



Sky had originally pursued a Norwich Pharmacal order, which allows information on individuals who have become 'mixed up' in wrongdoing to be obtained. Sky’s representative, Theo Donnelly, told the High Court that any details and information obtained would be used to take legal action against resellers and some of the users.

‌



Revolut counsel John Freeman said his client was neutral on the Sky application. The company’s fresh move comes as a result of a civil prosecution against David Dunbar of Wexford.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

FIVE Baller League footballers now punished by clubs as new sack statement drops READ MORE :

He was ordered to pay Sky €480,000 (£415,000) in damages and €100,000 (£86,000) in costs, having been found to have operated an illegal streaming service. Dunbar was fined a further €30,000 (£26,000) for contempt over a breach of court orders to preserve data.

‌



It was also found that 12 resellers and 304 users had been transferring sums to Dunbar by Revolut, which triggered Sky into requesting further information from the bank. Proceedings had already been issued against two of the 12 resellers, the court heard.

Information on the 304 users is due to be handed over to Sky on an encrypted spreadsheet within the next 28 days.

The use of so-called ‘dodgy boxes' and illegal Fire stick devices are deemed as 'serious crimes', and police forces across the UK and Ireland, alongside the Federation Against Copyright Theft (FACT) have been attempting to crack down on usage.

‌



View 2 Images Fans are using illegal boxes to watch Premier League games (Image: Getty Images)

A man from Merseyside was recently hit with a prison sentence for his own personal use.

Speaking at the time, Detective Sergeant Adam Dagnall of Merseyside Police ’s Cybercrime Unit said: "Merseyside Police takes illegal streaming seriously because it often helps fund organised crime and puts users at real risk.

‌



"Not only is it a criminal offence, but the unauthorised streaming services frequently contain malware that can compromise personal devices and lead to the loss of information such as banking details, or be used to commit identity fraud.

"Legitimate providers have security measures that protect users from these threats. Please don’t be tempted by cheap so-called ‘fully loaded’ or ‘jailbroken’ devices, even as Christmas gifts – you could end up getting more than you bargained for."

Football.title: Harper condemns 'rude' Dubois: 'I don’t like bullies'
Football.content:
Terri Harper insists their growing enmity will only fuel her in her fight against Caroline Dubois.

Harper and Dubois put their WBO and WBC lightweight world championships on the line in their clash, live on Sky Sports on Sunday April 5.

They have exchanged fierce words throughout the build up to their unification and Harper has condemned her rival champion.

"I don't like rude people. I don't like bullies. That's the vibe she gives me. She just rubs me up the wrong way," she told Sky Sports News.

"It's great though because that's what I need. Because I probably am a bit laid back.

"She gives me that fire in my belly that I need. I actually want to go out there and do the work on her.

"For me there's no pressure, Caroline's the one who's been running her mouth."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Caroline Dubois vows to bring back Terri Harper's nightmares during their world title unification clash on April 5.

She added: "I'm glad she's had a lot to say. She's done the dirty work for us and she's selling the fight. It would have been quite hard if we were both quiet and polite like myself. Caroline's done the work now and it's time for me to do the work on [the] Sunday night."

The reigning WBO belt-holder at 135lbs, Harper is a seasoned three-weight world champion. She believes she has a crucial advantage against her younger opponent.

"I feel like as a fighter I've experienced probably everything you can experience. A broken hand, cuts, pull outs the mornings of fights, as a fighter I've got a lot under my belt, I've got a lot of experience and I know I'm going to be able to use that to my advantage on the night," Harper said.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Caroline Dubois and Terri Harper went head-to-head on the Gloves Are Off.

"It's huge, it's my advantage. I've experienced everything and on the night you kind of know how to play the rounds in certain scenarios so I'm just going to use it all to my advantage."

She believes she will zero in on a weakness in Dubois' armoury.

"She will tire, 100 percent. The pattern's set, you can see it," Harper declared. "Caroline going over to America for three weeks' training, it's not going to miraculously improve her fitness. I've just got to focus on myself and do what I do best.

"You can see that she fades the second half of the fight so I'd be quite surprised if she carried that power through."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Caroline Dubois repeated her claim that she considers Terri Harper 'irrelevant' and says victory on April 5th is just the start of her boxing legacy.

Harper is anticipating a landmark night in her own career.

"It's everything I've dreamed of from being a young amateur," she said.

"I've just been that focused on the job I've got to do. Once it's all been and gone I can sit back and realise what I've done."

Football.title: Gauff dismantles Muchova to reach Miami final as Sinner sweeps past Tiafoe
Football.content:
Coco Gauff ruthlessly dismantled Karolina Muchova to reach the final of the Miami Open.

Muchova actually took a break to win the opening game. But from then it became a struggle for the Czech player to so much as register a game on the board.

Enjoying a home tournament in Florida, Gauff would not be denied. She stormed to victory in straight sets, winning 6-1 6-1 as she closed the match out with yet another break of serve.

Image: Coco Gauff has reached the final of the Miami Open (Associated Press)

It was her first straight sets victory in this tournament with Gauff hitting her stride now in the late stages of the competition.

"It feels great to be at home," Gauff told Sky Sports Tennis. "Today I was able to find that good balance of mixing it up.

"When I stepped on court today I felt like it was going to be a really strong match.

"I was just trying to fight for every point and we ended up with the scoreline."

She will meet the winner of Aryna Sabalenka and Elena Rybakina in the final on Saturday.

Sinner eases past Tiafoe

Jannik Sinner cruised past Frances Tiafoe to reach the semi-finals of the Miami Open.

Image: Jannik Sinner, of Italy, celebrates a point. (AP Photo/Jim Rassol)

Sinner further extended his record for consecutive sets won at ATP Masters 1000 events to 30 as he swept past Tiafoe 6-2 6-2.

World No 2 Sinner - who had surpassed Novak Djokovic's long-standing previous mark of 24 earlier in the tournament - never looked back after breaking Tiafoe's serve in the opening game to dampen the partisan home crowd with a dominant display at Hard Rock Stadium.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the Miami Open quarter-final match between Jannik Sinner and Frances Tiafoe.

"I feel like starting ⁠the match is very important. When you start by going a ‌break up, it gives you already a slightly good confidence," said Sinner, who won the title in Indian Wells without dropping a set.

"At the same ‌time, they might be very aggressive, so I try to stay as compact as possible with my shots.

"Also mentally, there can be some ⁠ups and downs on both sides, ​so I try to stay very ​calm and take the chances I can ​get. I think today that was the ⁠key."

The Italian will play either world No 3 Alexander Zverev or Francisco Cerundolo for a place in the final.

Watch the ATP and WTA Tours live on Sky Sports, with the Miami Open running until March 29. Stream contract-free with NOW.

Football.title: Tuchel: White must 'clear the air' with England team-mates after ending exile
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel says Ben White must "clear the air" with his England team-mates as he defended calling up the Arsenal defender after he ended his self-imposed international exile.

White left the 2022 World Cup in Qatar due to personal reasons after not featuring in the group stage matches. Following a disagreement with the then assistant manager, Steve Holland, he declined a call up by Gareth Southgate for the international break in March 2024.

However, he made it clear to Tuchel he would be available for selection again and has been brought into this camp as a replacement for the injured Jarell Quansah, with England facing Uruguay in a friendly at Wembley on Friday night before hosting Japan on Tuesday.

Tuchel says White will now have to talk with the team-mates who were part of that World Cup squad and in this camp.

"First of all, I think everyone deserves a second chance," said Tuchel. "Second, I don't know exactly what happened. I was also not 100 per cent interested because I wanted to open a new page and a new book and a new opportunity.

"Once I asked Ben if he would be ready to play for me and for England, he straightaway, without hesitation, said he would love to come back and was desperate to come back.

Amended England squad Goalkeepers: Dean Henderson*, Jordan Pickford, James Trafford, Aaron Ramsdale, Jason Steele.



Defenders: Dan Burn*, Marc Guehi*, Lewis Hall, Ezri Konsa*, Tino Livramento, Harry Maguire, Nico O'Reilly*, Ben White, Djed Spence, John Stones, Fikayo Tomori.



Midfielders: Elliot Anderson*, Jude Bellingham, James Garner, Jordan Henderson, Kobbie Mainoo, Declan Rice*, Morgan Rogers*, Adam Wharton.



Forwards: Jarrod Bowen, Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Harvey Barnes, Phil Foden, Anthony Gordon*, Harry Kane*, Noni Madueke, Cole Palmer, Marcus Rashford, Bukayo Saka*, Dominic Solanke.



*11 players will not join up with the England squad until after Friday's game with Uruguay

"[When he got the call after Quansah's injury] The reaction came within seconds. It was very euphoric, very positive and very emotional about it. That showed me he really means it.

"The way he behaves and the way he has trained here in camp has been exceptional, very, very good. It shows his quality.

"I think it's necessary he clears the air with his team-mates and I think he will with players who come back who were in the World Cup together with him.

"Then we go from there. Let's see how it goes and let's see how he feels, if he feels as comfortable and as confident as he thought he would. Until now, this is the case.

"He has my support, hopefully he gets the support from the spectators and we take it from there."

Why was White picked over Alexander-Arnold?

Image: Trent Alexander-Arnold has been overlooked by Thomas Tuchel

Tuchel was also quizzed on why he continues to overlook Trent Alexander-Arnold, with White preferred as a right-back replacement for Quansah than the Real Madrid defender.

His answer finished with a blunt instruction to Alexander-Arnold: "He just has to accept it."

Tuchel said: "It's just a choice. A sportive choice. Maybe a hard choice. And maybe to a degree unfair," said Tuchel.

"The choice was for White because I saw him play in the [Carabao Cup] final, in the Champions League match before and he was back to his old self. It was a chance to meet him in person.

"I know what Trent can give us so the choice was for Ben and Tino Livramento, to stick with the guys who had a good camp with us in September, November, December. Djed Spence as well.

"I know it creates noise when you leave a player like Trent out. We had a call, I tried to explain the situation. He just has to accept it."

'Positive pressure' - Tuchel on a chance impress

With this being the final England camp before Tuchel names his England squad for the World Cup, Tuchel conceded the was pressure on players to show what they can do.

And with 11 regulars not joining the camp until after Friday's friendly with Uruguay, there is a big opportunity for fringe players to step up.

"It's a good atmosphere. We addressed it right away that it's the last camp and encouraged everyone to be the best version of themselves," said Tuchel about the situation.

"We try to build a team effort, we demand a positive attitude, that everyone tries to connect as quick as possible.

"We are ready to have a good match [on Friday]. Will everything work smoothly on the pitch? Probably not. But that's the situation they're in.

"It's a positive pressure because they fight for England and a ticket to the World Cup.

"Decisions will be made later in May. [Friday] is just a chance to impress."

Follow England vs Uruguay on Sky Sports' digital platforms on Friday; kick-off 7.45pm

Football.title: Sky Sports receives five nominations for SJA Sports Journalism awards
Football.content:
Sky Sports have picked up five nominations at this year's SJA British Sports Journalism awards with Kaveh Solhekol, Craig Slater, Adam Bate and Megan Wellens all in contention.

It will be the 49th annual staging of the awards, which date back to 1976 when industry legends Hugh McIlvanney and Ian Wooldridge were jointly named the inaugural winners of the SJA Sportswriter of the Year Trophy.

With the digital direction of sports media, the Sports Publisher category brings together organisations regardless of platform, format or distribution model.

There are 10 contenders for the crown, including Sky Sports and The Athletic, the most recent winners of the equivalent category for content, and Telegraph Sport and CNN Sports, both recognised for their digital publishing acumen within the last five years.

Daily Mail, ITV Sport, talkSPORT, The Guardian, Times Sport and TNT Sports complete the shortlist.

For the first time, the feature writer award is split into long-form and short-form sub-categories, while darts, F1 and tennis are among the sports represented in Specialist Correspondent.

Football.title: 'Everyone deserves a second chance' - Tuchel explains White decision
Football.content:
Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player

Thomas Tuchel speaks on recalling Ben White to the England set-up ahead of friendlies against Uruguay and Japan after he made himself unavailable for selection under Gareth Southgate.

Football.title: Which players will seize their chance to impress Tuchel?
Football.content:
England's friendly against Uruguay is an invaluable opportunity for players pushing for a place in Thomas Tuchel's World Cup squad.

With 11 of the most established members of his experimental 35-man group rested before Tuesday's meeting with Japan, including Marc Guehi, Declan Rice, Elliot Anderson, Bukayo Saka and Harry Kane, there are openings throughout the team for others.

As England face Uruguay, and Tuchel takes a first-hand look at his options in the final camp before they jet out to the tournament in June, which players will seize the chance to impress?

Amended England squad Goalkeepers: Dean Henderson*, Jordan Pickford, James Trafford, Aaron Ramsdale, Jason Steele.



Defenders: Dan Burn*, Marc Guehi*, Lewis Hall, Ezri Konsa*, Tino Livramento, Harry Maguire, Nico O'Reilly*, Ben White, Djed Spence, John Stones, Fikayo Tomori.



Midfielders: Elliot Anderson*, Jude Bellingham, James Garner, Jordan Henderson, Kobbie Mainoo, Declan Rice*, Morgan Rogers*, Adam Wharton.



Forwards: Jarrod Bowen, Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Harvey Barnes, Phil Foden, Anthony Gordon*, Harry Kane*, Noni Madueke, Cole Palmer, Marcus Rashford, Bukayo Saka*, Dominic Solanke.



*11 players will not join up with the England squad until after Friday's game with Uruguay

Point to prove for Maguire?

Harry Maguire's chances of making the squad for a fourth major tournament appeared remote as recently as a couple of months ago but his resurgence under Michael Carrick earned him his first call-up in 18 months. "It was amazing to get the phone call," he said.

The 33-year-old was sent off against Bournemouth after hearing of his inclusion but his form, that lapse aside, has put him in a strong position as an experienced option alongside Guehi and Ezri Konsa, both of whom are being rested against Uruguay.

John Stones provides the option of another experienced head at centre-back but unlike Maguire, who has started last 10 games for Manchester United, Stones has been a peripheral figure for his club. He remains unreliable in terms of his fitness, with only two Manchester City starts under his belt since November.

Tuchel, though, has the option of pairing them together against Uruguay for the first time since England's friendly defeat to Brazil in March 2024, albeit with Fikayo Tomori as an alternative.

Given Guehi and Konsa appear to be Tuchel's preferred options in what has come to look like an area of renewal during his tenure, Friday's game may present a chance for Maguire and his old England centre-back partner to show they still have value.

Mainoo, Garner pushing?

There seems little doubt that Anderson and Rice will go to the tournament as starters but Tuchel can use the Uruguay game to look at other midfield options. He knows what Jordan Henderson can bring. He has looked at Adam Wharton before too. But what about Kobbie Mainoo and James Garner?

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Take a look at Kobbie Mainoo's performance for Manchester United against Manchester City in the Premier League

Mainoo has 10 England caps but none under Tuchel, having not featured for the national team since September 2024. Similarly to Maguire, his international prospects have been transformed by the arrival of Carrick in place of Ruben Amorim at Manchester United.

Mainoo has started every game under Carrick and Tuchel has clearly been watching. "I see his quality, so I want to meet him in person and see what he can do to get to know him. Competition is on for him."

One of the players he is competing with is the uncapped Garner, who received glowing praise from Tuchel after his call-up. The England boss described him as an "underrated" player and "fantastic team-mate" who stands out both physically and technically.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player James Garner's best goals and assists for Everton in the Premier League this season

Tuchel also cited his versatility, a quality which may give him the edge on other midfield options, providing he can make the most of his opportunity. Garner has featured at right-back and left-back for Everton this season, as well as on both sides of midfield.

A European Championship winner with England's U21s in 2023, he will be eager for a chance to show what he can offer against Uruguay.

Opportunity for Palmer and Foden?

Phil Foden has enjoyed a positive week in front of the dartboard, stunning team-mates with a 180 during Luke Littler's visit to the England camp. Now his task is to hit the target against Uruguay.

The 25-year-old has a curiously underwhelming international record having only scored four times in 47 appearances. As such, he ranks as an outside bet in the battle to be considered England's first-choice No 10 despite his undoubted talent.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player England and Manchester City midfielder Phil Foden stuns team-mates with a 180 on the dartboad. Video credit: @England

England have enviable depth in the position but with Eberechi Eze injured, Morgan Rogers rested, and Jude Bellingham still building up his fitness, the Uruguay game could be key to his hopes of forcing his way back to the forefront of his manager's thinking.

He is competing with another player enduring a difficult season in Cole Palmer, who excelled as an impact substitute during England's run to the Euro 2024 final but has struggled to kick on since.

Time for Calvert-Lewin?

Ollie Watkins impressed as England's alternative to Kane at Euro 2024 but his omission from Tuchel's squad has opened the door to others. The England boss says he is using the camp to get a "better picture" of Dominic Calvert-Lewin and Dominic Solanke.

Calvert-Lewin has not played for England since 2021 and admitted a return to the international fold felt "a million miles away" during his injury struggles but his rejuvenation at Leeds this season has thrust him back into contention.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Ollie Watkins, Danny Welbeck and Dominic Calvert-Lewin are in form this season - but who will be selected for England to join Harry Kane at next summer's World Cup?

The 29-year-old has scored 10 goals in 28 Premier League games for Daniel Farke's side. Crucially, he has also avoided any notable fitness setbacks, allowing him to demonstrate his quality as a targetman whose runs in behind offer something different to Kane.

Tuchel also has the option of fielding Marcus Rashford centrally against Uruguay but Calvert-Lewin and Solanke are the ones with something to prove before Kane's expected return against Japan.

Follow England vs Uruguay on Sky Sports' digital platforms on Friday; kick-off 7.45pm

Football.title: World Cup 2026 playoffs: Wales v Bosnia-Herzegovina, Czech Republic v Republic of Ireland and more – live
Football.content:
3m ago 17.55 EDT Wales 1-1 Bosnia-Herzegovina: Now it’s Bosnia-Herzegovina’s turn to threaten … Demirovic is slipped in inside the right … but the angle is too tight as he finds the sidenetting. Share

6m ago 17.52 EDT Wales 1-1 Bosnia-Herzegovina: Harry Wilson’s shot from near the penalty spot is heading for the net … but Muharemovic pulls off a ridiculous header to save his side. Share

12m ago 17.45 EDT Poland advance Poland hold on to complete their comeback against Albania, winning 2-1. They’ll face Sweden on Tuesday. Lewandowski v Gyökeres, here we come. Share

15m ago 17.43 EDT Extra-time ... Wales are level with Bosnia-Herzegovina, as are Ireland with Czech Republic. We’re going to extra-time in those two games. Share

16m ago 17.42 EDT Denmark, Kosovo and Sweden advance Full-time scores: Denmark 4-0 North Macedonia

Slovakia 3-4 Kosovo

Ukraine 1-3 Sweden View image in fullscreen Kosovo win a thriller in Bratislava. Photograph: Joe Klamar/AFP/Getty Images Share Updated at 17.50 EDT

21m ago 17.37 EDT Christopher Faherty writes in: “Tough not to say the Czechs deserve it. All we’ve done from the half-hour mark is try and kill the game really.” Share

23m ago 17.34 EDT Ladislav Krejci, on loan at Wolves, pops up at the near post from a corner to equalise for the hosts. Remember, they were 2-0 down. Share

25m ago 17.32 EDT GOAL! Czech Republic 2-2 Republic of Ireland (Krejci 86) And now it’s Ireland who are staring at extra-time. View image in fullscreen Ladislav Krejci! Photograph: Sebastian Widmann/UEFA/Getty Images Share Updated at 17.38 EDT

26m ago 17.31 EDT GOAL! Wales 1-1 Bosnia-Herzegovina (Dzeko 86) Oh my. It’s the captain, the former Inter and Manchester City man: Edin Dzeko heads in from an inswinging corner and Bosnia-Herzegovina are alive. View image in fullscreen Edin Dzeko! Photograph: Robbie Jay Barratt/AMA/Getty Images Share Updated at 17.36 EDT

28m ago 17.30 EDT Wales 1-0 Bosnia-Herzegovina: With six minutes of normal time left, Dan James leaves the field. He missed on penalties when Wales lost their Euro 2024 playoff against Poland. He could be the hero tonight. Share

30m ago 17.28 EDT GOAL! Denmark 4-0 North Macedonia (Nørgaard 76) You can take the man out of Arsenal … Christian Nørgaard is on the end of a corner for Denmark. Share

33m ago 17.25 EDT GOAL! Poland 2-1 Albania (Zielinski 73) It’s a piledriver to complete the turnaround! Piotr Zielinski picks the ball up in midfield and lasers an effort from more than 25 yards out, finding the bottom left corner. View image in fullscreen Piotr Zielinski scores for Poland! Photograph: Adam Nurkiewicz/UEFA/Getty Images Share Updated at 17.31 EDT

36m ago 17.22 EDT GOAL! Ukraine 0-3 Sweden (Gyökeres pen 72) A spotkick seals a hat-trick for Viktor Gyökeres – Sweden are all set for a final with Poland or Albania. View image in fullscreen Big Vik hat-trick for Sweden. Photograph: Jonas Ekströmer/TT/Shutterstock Share Updated at 17.26 EDT

38m ago 17.20 EDT GOAL! Poland 1-1 Albania (Lewandowski 63) Robert Lewandowski, 37, is still up for another World Cup – the Barcelona man rises at the far post to head in an inswinging corner. View image in fullscreen Bob Lewandowski levels it for Poland. Photograph: Kacper Pempel/Reuters Share Updated at 17.25 EDT

40m ago 17.17 EDT GOAL! Slovakia 2-4 Kosovo (Hajrizi 72) Kreshnik Hajrizi is there to smash home a rebound – Kosovo are closing on a meeting with Turkey! Share

44m ago 17.14 EDT GOAL! Slovakia 2-3 Kosovo (Muslija 60) Kosovo take the lead for the first time! Florent Muslija’s free-kick gets over the wall and dips in to the bottom left corner. What a momentous night this could be for them, a side who have never been to a major tournament. View image in fullscreen Florent Muslija scores for Kosovo! Photograph: Christian Hofer/UEFA/Getty Images Share Updated at 17.17 EDT

47m ago 17.11 EDT Wales 1-0 Bosnia-Herzegovina: Karl Darlow has just made the most outrageous save for Wales, diving to his left to claw a header out from Demirovic. Share Updated at 17.11 EDT

49m ago 17.08 EDT GOALS! Denmark 3-0 North Macedonia (Iskasen 58 & 59) All of a sudden, this one’s pretty much done. Gustav Isaksen gets two in two minutes, the second a fine rebound strike. Share

52m ago 17.05 EDT GOAL! Ukraine 0-2 Sweden (Gyökeres 51) The striker has his and Sweden’s second: in the left channel, he cuts in and delivers a lovely, precise finish into the bottom right corner. View image in fullscreen Sweden are in the driving seat. Photograph: Pablo Morano/Reuters Share Updated at 17.08 EDT

55m ago 17.03 EDT GOAL! Denmark 1-0 North Macedonia (Damsgaard 49) Mikkel Damsgaard just has a defender beat with his strike, the keeper out of the picture after a scrappy bit of play in the North Macedonia area. View image in fullscreen Brentford man Mikkel Damsgaard puts Denmark ahead. Photograph: Liselotte Sabroe/Ritzau Scanpix/AFP/Getty Images Share Updated at 17.06 EDT

1h ago 16.58 EDT It’s a an absolute cracker. A wayward kick from the visitors finds James out on the right, he heads the ball down with his first touch but he’s still 25 yards out from a tight angle … he hits the bouncing ball so, so sweetly to beat the keeper. What a hit from the Leeds winger. Share

1h ago 16.53 EDT GOAL! Slovakia 2-2 Kosovo (Asllani 47) This one keeps on delivering! Hoffenheim’s Fisnik Asllani pulls off a delightful glancing header to find the top right corner for Kosovo. View image in fullscreen What a game in Bratislava! Photograph: Radovan Stoklasa/Reuters Share Updated at 16.58 EDT

1h ago 16.41 EDT Wales 0-0 Bosnia-Herzegovina: Harry Wilson came closest for Wales in that half, a left-footed curling effort from the right smacking the post. Share

1h ago 16.38 EDT Half-time scores Czech Republic 1-2 Republic of Ireland

Denmark 0-0 North Macedonia

Poland 0-1 Albania

Slovakia 2-1 Kosovo

Ukraine 0-1 Sweden

Wales 0-0 Bosnia-Herzegovina Share

1h ago 16.35 EDT GOAL! Slovakia 2-1 Kosovo (Haraslin 45) Slovakia retake the lead before half-time: Lukas Haraslin’s free-kick from the left is teasing ... and it beats everyone, befuddling the keeper to sneak in at the far post. View image in fullscreen Lukas Haraslin restores the lead for Slovakia. Photograph: Joe Klamar/AFP/Getty Images Share Updated at 16.46 EDT

1h ago 16.33 EDT GOAL! Poland 0-1 Albania (Hoxha 42) Albania, managed by former Arsenal full-back Sylvinho and yet to reach a World Cup, have the lead. Arber Hoxha capitalises on a mistake at the back to round the keeper and slot in. View image in fullscreen Arber Hoxha puts Albania in front against Poland in Warsaw. Photograph: Kacper Pempel/Reuters Share Updated at 16.45 EDT

1h ago 16.29 EDT Matt Dony writes in: double quotation mark Wales went through a tricky period of transition. Any team in the world would suffer from the loss of Bale, but Ramsey and Allen have been big losses, too. Bellamy has been tremendous, much better than I expected, and he’s galvanised them into a real ‘team’ again. The 7-1 against North Macedonia in the last game was magnificent, but sadly overshadowed by Scotland turning into prime Brazil on the same night. I honestly don’t think they’ll end up qualifying for the World Cup, which is disappointing, but I think the next few years could be fun. Well, they’re really going for it at the moment. Harry Wilson, in the 40th minute, tries to catch the keeper out with a free-kick from the right, forcing a decent save at the near post. Share

2h ago 16.21 EDT GOAL! Slovakia 1-1 Kosovo (Hodza 21) After all the carnage at the Fortuna Arena, let’s go to Bratislava, where Kosovo have equalised. Velin Hodza, from the edge of the area, curls a first-time shot into the bottom left corner. View image in fullscreen Veldin Hodza equalises for Kosovo! Photograph: Dénes Erdős/AP Share Updated at 16.31 EDT

2h ago 16.17 EDT It’s a clear tug of the shirt in the area by Manning, a needless foul, and the hosts have a lifeline. Patrik Schick beats Kelleher and we’ve got a proper game. Share

2h ago 16.15 EDT GOAL! Czech Republic 1-2 Republic of Ireland (Schick pen 27) Well, this is just wild. View image in fullscreen Game on! Photograph: Filip Singer/EPA Share Updated at 16.22 EDT

2h ago 16.15 EDT It’s a corner from the right for Ireland, delivered by Manning … O’Shea heads it into the mixer from the back post – Coufal’s defensive header finds the bar and rebounds off the keeper into the net. Pinball goes Ireland’s way. The visiting bench erupts in joy. Share Updated at 16.23 EDT

2h ago 16.12 EDT GOAL! Czech Republic 0-2 Republic of Ireland (Kovar og 23) Another one! View image in fullscreen Ireland are in dream land! Photograph: David W Černý/Reuters Share Updated at 16.16 EDT

2h ago 16.11 EDT A kick on Nathan Collins prompts the referee to head to the monitor. He awards the spotkick and Troy Parrott finds the left despite the keeper guessing the right way. Share

2h ago 16.06 EDT GOAL! Czech Republic 0-1 Republic of Ireland (Parrott 19 pen) It’s that man again! View image in fullscreen Troy Parrott fires home and Ireland lead in Prague! Photograph: Sebastian Widmann/UEFA/Getty Images Share Updated at 16.09 EDT

2h ago 16.05 EDT Wales 0-0 Bosnia-Herzegovina: David Brooks bursts through in the area to win a corner … Wilson drives the ball to the far post, Jordan James heads into the mixer but Bosnia-Herzegovina handle the danger. Share Updated at 16.42 EDT

2h ago 15.59 EDT GOAL! Slovakia 1-0 Kosovo (Valjent 6) Slovakia, bidding for their first World Cup appearance since 2010, take an early lead through Mallorca’s Martin Valjent. The free-kick comes from the right, and the centre-back rises for a powerful header. View image in fullscreen Martin Valjent scores for Slovakia. Photograph: Radovan Stoklasa/Reuters Share Updated at 16.04 EDT

2h ago 15.55 EDT GOAL! Ukraine 0-1 Sweden (Gyökeres 6) The Arsenal striker taps in! Anthony Elanga slips the ball for Benjamin Nygren inside the area, and his ball across goal is perfect. Gyökeres just needs to get a touch before getting his hands up for that trademark celebration. Graham Potter’s boys have the perfect start. View image in fullscreen Big Vik pokes home and Sweden lead. Photograph: Joel Marklund/BILDBYRÅN/Shutterstock Share Updated at 15.57 EDT

2h ago 15.50 EDT Wales 0-0 Bosnia-Herzegovina: The hosts have an early free-kick on the right, with Harry Wilson over it … it’s flicked on at the near post but doesn’t pose any problems for Bosnia-Herzegovina. Dan James is looking lively already for Wales. Share Updated at 16.42 EDT

2h ago 15.43 EDT Ben Davies, missing for Wales due to injury, is presented with a golden cap to mark his 100 appearances for the national team. Share

2h ago 15.41 EDT Bob Walnut has sent me his Saipan review on Letterboxd: three and a half stars. “Far more abstract and arty than I was expecting.” It goes on my list. There’s a light show on at Cardiff City Stadium as we get ready for kick-off. Ethan Ampadu leads Wales out and Edin Dzeko – still going, banging them in for Schalke – is the frontman for Bosnia-Herzegovina. Share

3h ago 15.12 EDT This is how things are set up for Tuesday: Wales/Bosnia-Herzegovina v Italy/Northern Ireland

Ukraine/Sweden v Poland/Albania

Slovakia/Kosovo v Turkey

Czech Republic/Republic of Ireland v Denmark/North Macedonia Share

3h ago 15.05 EDT As we prepare for Czech Republic against the Republic of Ireland … can anyone tell me whether Saipan is worth a watch? Share

View image in fullscreen Turkey progress to the play-off final. Photograph: Yasin Akgül/AFP/Getty Images Share Updated at 15.06 EDT

Football.title: Italy 2-0 Northern Ireland: World Cup playoff – live reaction
Football.content:
3m ago 17.55 EDT Shea Charles, trying his hardest to look on the bright side, has a word with the Beeb. “Obviously there’s disappointment [in the dressing room] … I thought we played well … we can hold our heads high … we’re still a young team … we have a long way to go … it’s about how we bounce back … we need a bit more composure … we made a couple of mistakes … being able to survive little periods … proud of the boys … we had a few chances and on a different night we take them … I back myself against anyone … I thought I had a good game tonight … we’re all growing together … push onto the Nations League in September.” Share

7m ago 17.52 EDT Italy hero Moise Kean, a handful all night, talks to the BBC. “The emotion is big … especially when you play with a full stadium … always exciting … now we have the last game and we hope to do great … [Northern Ireland] are a great team … the first half was a little bit tough … tight … I am happy to help the team.” Share

13m ago 17.46 EDT Stand-in captain Trai Hume, understandably downbeat, speaks to the BBC. “Disappointing … we were good in the game overall … managed them well … they had their moments and unfortunately they took them … we were breaking and creating chances but missing the final pass … obviously the goal changed the dynamic … I can’t speak my emotion too much right now … it’s been a dream … the fans even after defeat are amazing … I’d like to thank them for coming out … it’s a good experience to go through … we’re a young team and we’re only going to get better.” Share

16m ago 17.42 EDT Michael O’Neill and Gennaro Gattuso embrace on the touchline. Some genuine respect between both men by the looks of their friendly back-and-forth. O’Neill and his players have certainly earned it. They made Italy work hard for their win. And Italy had to work hard, because they were battling both the Irish and their own nerves. But seven attempts on target to nil tells its own story, and the pressure told: Sandro Tonali and Moise Kean scored a cracker apiece to book the Azzurri’s place in the qualification decider on Tuesday. Share

20m ago 17.38 EDT FULL TIME: Italy 2-0 Northern Ireland Italy will travel to either Wales or Bosnia-Herzegovina for the decider on Tuesday! Share
